advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `assert "[truncated]" not in state["prompts"][1]` to the under-cap test so the AC's 'marker only when the prior output exceeded the cap' clause is actually verified.
evidence: Ticket d5f0 AC: "Asserts prior output is present, bounded to the cap, and `[truncated]` appears only when the prior output exceeded the cap."

test_reask_echoes_the_models_own_faulty_prior_reply uses `faulty` (~323 chars, under the 2000 cap) but asserts only `faulty in state["prompts"][1]` and `out["verdict"] == "PASS"` — no assertion on the absence of `[truncated]` (tests/unit/test_silent_success.py:135-146)
runner.py:577-578 only appends the marker when `len(faulty) > _FAULTY_OUTPUT_SNIPPET_CHARS`, so the 'only when necessary' branch is the untested half

test body uses `faulty = "not valid json " * 20 + "REASK_ECHO_SENTINEL_XYZ"` (~324 chars, well under the 2000 cap) but only asserts `faulty in state["prompts"][1]` and `out["verdict"] == "PASS"` — no assertion that `[truncated]` is absent (tests/unit/test_silent_success.py:135,144-146)
runner truncation is CONDITIONAL: `if len(faulty) > _FAULTY_OUTPUT_SNIPPET_CHARS: faulty = faulty[:_FAULTY_OUTPUT_SNIPPET_CHARS] + " …[truncated]"` (src/rebar/llm/runner.py:577-578)

ticket d5f0-b85f-aa56-46b0 AC: 'Asserts prior output is present, bounded to the cap, and `[truncated]` appears only when the prior output exceeded the cap.' — the 'only when' clause is not covered by either test
advisory.finding: The ticket AC requires that the `[truncated]` marker appear ONLY when the prior output exceeded the cap, but only the positive case (oversized reply → marker present) is asserted; the negative case (a small under-cap reply must NOT gain a `[truncated]` marker) is never checked, so a regression that always appended the marker would pass.
merged_from: The below-cap reask test never asserts the absence of the '[truncated]' marker, leaving the conditional half of the truncation contract ('appears only when the prior output exceeded the cap') untested — a regression that unconditionally appends the marker (or truncates below the cap) would pass both tests.
advisory.suggested_fix: In test_reask_echoes_the_models_own_faulty_prior_reply, after asserting `faulty in state["prompts"][1]`, add `assert "[truncated]" not in state["prompts"][1]` (the ~323-char reply is under the 2000 cap, so no marker should be emitted).

coaching.coaching: Add a regression test covering the absent-marker case for a small faulty reply.
coaching.subject: the absent-marker case for a small faulty reply
